status: C-15 OPEN — candidate ChaCha20-PRF Extract; not cryptographically closed

prf_key_derivation.notes: tag is the ASCII string without NUL. seed_w_le32 is Bitcoin uint256 memory order (little-endian blob): GetHex/FromHex display is byte-reversed vs bytes.fromhex().

endian_notes.seed_w_hex: Bitcoin GetHex/FromHex display order (MSB digits first). Memory bytes = reverse(bytes.fromhex(hex)).
endian_notes.prf_key_bytes: SHA256 digest written in natural byte order; used as ChaCha20 key via eight LE32 words (RFC8439 / crypto/chacha20.h).
endian_notes.chacha_nonce96: nonce_first = uint32(raw) XOR lane; nonce_second = (uint64(i)<<32) | uint64(j). Seek(Nonce96{nonce_first, nonce_second}, remix) with remix as block counter.
endian_notes.keystream_le64: First 8 keystream bytes interpreted as little-endian uint64 (AccelReplica returns x0 | (x1<<32) of the ChaCha block).
endian_notes.scale: e = scale_stream & 0x3; return exact_mul(mu, 1<<e) — never signed left-shift of a negative mantissa.

accel_replica_parity.note: ExtractDequantMatExpandAccelReplica must equal ExtractDequantMatExpand on these cases (and the broader grid in the C++ test).

rank_note: Honest B32 = (G·W)·H has rank(B32) ≤ w (=128 at production). Affine collapse of Extract would reopen a ~n/w Freivalds-style shortcut; see rank_spectral_regression.md.
